accept: docs/multi-env-design.md exists and evaluates each option on the criteria above with concrete examples.
Recommendation either (a) keep status quo with explicit rationale, or (b) implement one of B/C/D with implementation issue filed.
Multi-env story is concrete: 'agent A on laptop and agent B in Cowork both pick up issues from the same backlog' is a worked example end-to-end.
Forks-and-PRs implication is documented: when an outside contributor forks the repo, what does/doesn't come with the tracker state and is that the right default?
payload.description:
Andrew 2026-05-10: 'could you have act actions in some separate repo in the same folder? Is that viable/preferable, especially for multiple agents working across environments — local, remote, etc.?'

Today .act/ ops live in the project's git repo and ride the project's sync cadence. For multi-env scenarios (laptop + CC Web + Cowork agents all working the same backlog), the act state needs to sync between agents quickly so claims and closes are visible to all. Coupling .act/ sync to the project's main-branch sync mostly works but couples two cadences that don't naturally match.

Options to evaluate:

A) **Status quo**: .act/ in the project repo, in the working tree, committed normally.
B) **Separate-branch ops**: .act/ on refs/act/ops in the same repo (a la git notes). Project main stays clean; tracker state syncs via a different ref.
C) **Separate repo, same folder**: .act/ is its own git repo nested inside the project (or a sibling). Push/pull of tracker state independent of code. Could even use a different remote.
D) **Separate repo, separate folder**: .act/ lives outside the project entirely, e.g. ~/.act-state/<project-id>/. Maximum decoupling.

Compare on: setup cost; multi-env sync ease; how cleanly the audit trail correlates with code commits; tooling friction (does act doctor still work? does git log -- on the project show act activity?); story for forks/PRs (does cloning the project bring tracker state with it?).

Recommendation should land in docs/multi-env-design.md and feed an implementation issue if one of B/C/D wins. Status quo (A) is the deferral default; only switch if there's a clear win.
payload.title: research: separate-repo or separate-remote model for .act/ (multi-env distribution)
